domain: Welwitschia mirabilis basal meristem leaf growth (two leaves, base meristem, distal aging, never sheds; lifelong continuous record stored as continuous leaf-tissue strip)
specific_mechanism: Single basal meristem appends new tissue at one end while older tissue accumulates distally and gradually frays; no shedding or replacement; the whole history sits in one continuously-growing strip.
llm_application: WMCL: continual-learning architecture where base parameters are frozen; learning appends a NEW low-rank strip at a single fixed growth-edge (the meristem position in parameter space); previous strips are NEVER updated, NEVER removed; at inference, output is a monotonic distance-decayed weighted convolution over all historical strips. No replay, no merge, no orthogonal-subspace constraint, no router — just one-edge continuous append.
novelty_claim: O-LoRA / OA-Adapter (2505.22358) impose orthogonal subspaces per task. ELLA (2601.02232) uses subspace de-correlation. ELDER uses adapter routers. None pin growth to a single meristem-edge with monotonic distance-decay convolution over strip-stack and zero routing.
